summary: DHH announces that Ruby on Rails has been featured on Slashdot, a major tech news aggregator, marking an early milestone in Rails' visibility. The Slashdot story focused on KDevelop's integrated Ruby support but also mentioned Rails. DHH directs newcomers to key onboarding resources including a 10-minute demo video, a tutorial, and the Gem Rails installer. He highlights the welcoming IRC community of nearly a hundred members ready to help newcomers.
key_points: Rails reached the front page of Slashdot in late 2004, a significant early exposure milestone
DHH curates a clear onboarding path: watch a video, read a tutorial, then install and start building
The early Rails community was small (~100 people on IRC) but positioned itself as welcoming and patient
money_quotes: Rails made the cover of Slashdot today.
We're a happy bunch of almost a hundred willing to answer loads of questions with almost endless patience.
key_insight: This post captures a pivotal early moment in Rails' rise, when a Slashdot feature brought the framework its first wave of mainstream developer attention.
